sampleInput.campaign_message: Compare two text-only lunch creative concepts before creative review.
sampleInput.creative_a_description: A clean desk scene with lunch options resolved quickly and a short benefit-led caption.
sampleInput.creative_b_description: A team lunch moment with nutrition proof points and dependable delivery reassurance.
sampleInput.key_message: Healthy team lunch decisions can feel fast, reviewed, and dependable.
sampleInput.claim: Creative concepts are text-only planning assumptions, not verified production claims.
sampleInput.context: Reviewed offline Creative Comparison MVP sample for executive creative-review planning.

summary.headline: Offline creative comparison ready for executive review
summary.text: Creative A and Creative B are compared as text-only concepts using the same audience, platform mix, key message, and evidence rules. No winner is selected from synthetic evidence; the result is inconclusive planning input for human creative review.

creativeSummaries.title: Creative A summary
creativeSummaries.detail: Speed-led concept; review whether the visual/copy idea makes the benefit clear without overclaiming time savings.

creativeSummaries.title: Creative B summary
creativeSummaries.detail: Trust-proof concept; review whether reassurance and nutrition cues stay useful without implying verified outcomes.

cards.title: Audience fit
cards.detail: Working adults and urban consumers may understand both concepts; field evidence is not attached.

cards.title: Brand fit
cards.detail: Both concepts remain aligned to dependable lunch decisions and avoid production-ready claims.

cards.title: Message clarity
cards.detail: Use human review to decide whether speed or trust proof better supports the shared key message.

cards.title: Risk / caveats
cards.detail: Synthetic offline evidence is insufficient for winner selection, scaling, or conversion prediction.

cards.title: Evidence notes
cards.detail: No live APIs, uploads, image generation, CRM data, or private audience data are used.

comparisonCards.title: Comparison highlights
comparisonCards.detail: Creative A emphasizes quick resolution; Creative B emphasizes reassurance. Treat this as a review frame, not a winner call.

comparisonCards.title: Differentiators
comparisonCards.detail: The concepts differ in emphasis while sharing the same key message and platform assumptions.

comparisonCards.title: Decision Status
comparisonCards.detail: Current evidence is inconclusive; collect comparable creative feedback before deciding.

comparisonDashboard.title: Comparison highlights
comparisonDashboard.detail: Creative A is clearer on speed; Creative B is clearer on trust proof. Evidence remains synthetic and inconclusive.

comparisonDashboard.title: Differentiators
comparisonDashboard.detail: Creative A uses a clean task-resolution scene; Creative B uses a team proof/reassurance scene.

comparisonDashboard.title: Audience fit
comparisonDashboard.detail: Both concepts fit the stated audience directionally; no segment-level targeting or microtargeting is supported.

comparisonDashboard.title: Brand fit
comparisonDashboard.detail: Both concepts fit a helpful, practical brand tone; claims still need human substantiation review.

comparisonDashboard.title: Message clarity
comparisonDashboard.detail: Both concepts can support the key message; clarity is inconclusive without comparable field evidence.

comparisonDashboard.title: Risk / caveats
comparisonDashboard.detail: No winner selected; do not treat this as production advice, conversion prediction, or scaling guidance.

comparisonDashboard.title: Evidence notes
comparisonDashboard.detail: Offline fixture-backed sample using the closest existing marketing/experiment adapter path only.

comparisonDashboard.title: Recommended next action
comparisonDashboard.detail: Run a small human-reviewed creative feedback test with the same brief, audience, and platform assumptions.

comparisonMethod.rationale: Both creative concepts use the same brief, audience, platform mix, context, and criteria; synthetic evidence is inconclusive and cannot support winner selection.
comparisonMethod.parityCheck: Creative A and Creative B use the same key message, intended audience, platform mix, notes, and offline evidence basis.

platformBreakdown.detail: Planning cue only; not measured live platform activity.
Planning cue only; not measured live platform activity.

audienceInsights: Working Adults: compare speed clarity against confidence-building proof points.
Urban Consumers: review whether quick benefit framing or reassurance feels easier to understand.
SME Owners: keep team-benefit framing without promising productivity or conversion outcomes.
risksCaveats: Reviewed offline sample only; not production-ready and not connected to production campaign systems.
Not suitable for persuasion optimization, voter targeting, microtargeting, or guaranteed prediction claims.
Dashboard signals require human review before executive or public-facing use.
Offline workbench sample only; not a production simulator replacement.
Creative-specific SocialSense runtime scenario is not available; closest existing marketing/experiment path run_message_comparison is used for this offline fixture.
recommendedNextTest: Run a small human-reviewed creative feedback test with the same brief, audience, and platform assumptions before choosing a creative direction.
safetyLabels: No PII, CRM lists, private messages, or voter lists
No microtargeting, persuasion optimization, or conversion guarantees

exports.executiveSummaryPreview: Executive review: Creative A and Creative B are ready for directional human review. No winner is selected; use this to plan a small approved creative feedback test.

assumptions: Creative A title is Speed-first lunch creative.
Creative A description is A clean desk scene with lunch options resolved quickly and a short benefit-led caption.
Creative B title is Trust-proof lunch creative.
Creative B description is A team lunch moment with nutrition proof points and dependable delivery reassurance.
Key message is Healthy team lunch decisions can feel fast, reviewed, and dependable.
Creative Comparison MVP is text-only: no image generation, uploads, or live creative analysis.
Use only aggregate, synthetic, offline assumptions with no customer records or private audience data.
Creative concept reviewed: Speed-first lunch creative: A clean desk scene with lunch options resolved quickly and a short benefit-led caption.
The review contract was validated as offline/synthetic/demo-only before conversion.
Audience reactions are aggregate simulation signals, not measured public opinion or individual behavior.
No approved field data or backtest evidence is attached to this bridge output.
evidenceGaps: No real social platform APIs, private social scraping, private groups, CRM records, voter lists, or direct messages were used.
Treat outputs as synthetic simulation estimates unless separately validated with approved field data or backtests.
Offline sample mode uses deterministic sample data and is not evidence from the field.
limitations: Reviewed offline sample only; not production-ready and not connected to production campaign systems.
Not suitable for persuasion optimization, voter targeting, microtargeting, or guaranteed prediction claims.
Dashboard signals require human review before executive or public-facing use.
Offline workbench sample only; not a production simulator replacement.
Creative-specific SocialSense runtime scenario is not available; closest existing marketing/experiment path run_message_comparison is used for this offline fixture.

source.sourceLabel: Reviewed offline sample, no live data

reviewMetadata.uncertainty: Directional synthetic aggregate sample only; use for human review and next-test planning, not prediction.
